Title: PHR1-like 7 and phosphatidic acid oppositely regulate TAG degradation and seed oil accumulation in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed carbon and energy storage; supports germination and seedling establishment
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: expressed in developing seeds; strongly induced by PHL7 at 10-12 DAF
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: 12-DAF seeds showed strongest genotype-dependent differences; lower in phl7 and higher in PHL7-OE
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: SDP1 functions as a major TAG-degradation gene whose transcription is activated by PHL7, leading to lower seed oil/TAG levels
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development stage (10-12 DAF); transient tobacco leaf assay for promoter activation
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: native SDP1 promoter; long promoter fragment containing P1BS in reporter assay
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: lipidomics by ESI-MS/MS; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; ChIP-PCR showing PHL7 occupancy at SDP1 promoter; EMSA with SDP1 P1BS probe; transactivation assay in tobacco leaves; lipid phenotyping in PHL7 mutant/OE lines
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SDP1 transcript abundance was strongly decreased in phl7 and strongly increased in PHL7 overexpression seeds, especially at 12 DAF. 2. PHL7 bound the SDP1 promoter at a P1BS motif in vivo and in vitro. 3. PHL7 activated SDP1 promoter-driven luciferase expression, linking SDP1 upregulation to enhanced TAG degradation and reduced seed oil accumulation.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing SDP1 expression/activity is a practical route to increase seed oil yield; the study supports manipulating its upstream regulator PHL7 or the PHL7-SDP1 module for oilseed improvement.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: enhanced TAG degradation can accelerate germination but may reduce seedling growth when constitutively activated via PHL7 overexpression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although several lipases changed expression, SDP1 showed the largest response and was the only direct promoter-binding target validated in this study.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: central intermediate in TAG biosynthesis and lipid signaling mediator
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: developing seeds; likely nuclear PA also discussed
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: likely nucleus for functional interaction with PHL7, as discussed
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: PA levels gradually decreased during seed development; higher in phl7-1 and lower in PHL7-OE seeds
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PA antagonizes PHL7 activity, suppressing PHL7 binding to the SDP1 promoter and thereby attenuating TAG degradation to favor seed oil accumulation
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: specific PA molecular species tested in overlay, liposome-binding, and EMSA assays; unsaturated 18:1/18:2-containing PA species were effective
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: SDP1 promoter P1BS-containing fragment in EMSA context
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: lipidomics by ESI-MS/MS; no public accession reported
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: protein-lipid overlay assay; liposome-binding assay; EMSA competition assay; seed lipid profiling; genetic complementation with PA-binding-defective PHL7K61S
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHL7 specifically bound PA, especially unsaturated PA species containing 18:1 or 18:2 acyl chains. 2. PA inhibited PHL7 binding to the SDP1 promoter in EMSA. 3. PA-binding-defective PHL7K61S failed to restore normal seed oil phenotype in phl7, supporting functional importance of PA-PHL7 interaction.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Modulating nuclear PA or PA-PHL7 interaction may provide a lipid-signaling strategy to restrain TAG degradation and enhance seed oil accumulation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PA has broad signaling roles, so engineering PA pools may cause pleiotropic effects beyond seed oil metabolism.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PA is treated here as a central lipid regulator rather than a gene product; included because it is a primary experimentally tested core factor controlling the nutrient phenotype.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: WT; phl7-1 (CS882922); phl7-2 (SALK_034222); OE12; OE13; complementation lines Com1/5/9 and Com15/16/17/18
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: seed energy reserve for germination and early seedling growth
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: MYB-like helix-turn-helix (HTH) transcription factor; PHR1-like family; MYB-type HTH DNA-binding domain; coiled-coil/LHEQLE-containing region
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation of TAG degradation gene expression; activity is inhibited by lipid binding
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SDP1 (direct); other lipase genes including LIP1, MPL1, SAG101, LYSOPL2 changed transcriptionally but direct regulation not established
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: activates SDP1 expression via binding a P1BS motif in its promoter
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: conditionally decisive negative regulator of seed oil accumulation because it directly activates the key TAG lipase gene SDP1 during seed development
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: phosphatidic acid (PA), especially unsaturated PA species, binds PHL7 and suppresses its DNA-binding activity
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: possible metabolite-mediated feedback/homeostatic regulation, where PA generated within lipid metabolism attenuates PHL7 activation of SDP1
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: broadly expressed, with particularly high expression in flowers and seeds; expression increases at 10 DAF and peaks at 12 DAF
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in flowers and seeds; elevated during mid seed development; overexpression lines showed strong leaf expression from CaMV35S promoter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: seed development stage (6-14 DAF, strongest effects at 10-12 DAF); phosphate deficiency tested but no clear role in seedling P starvation response
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV35S constitutive promoter for overexpression and effector assays; native PHL7 promoter for complementation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: lipidomics by ESI-MS/MS; no public accession reported
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: T-DNA knockout; overexpression; native-promoter complementation with PHL7WT and PHL7K61S; qPCR; seed oil/TAG/FA/lipid profiling; protein-lipid overlay and liposome-binding assays; ChIP-PCR; EMSA; transactivation assay in tobacco leaves
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: PHL7 enhances TAG degradation and decreases seed oil accumulation; knockout raises seed oil by about 10%, whereas overexpression lowers oil by 8-13%
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PHL7 loss-of-function increased mature seed oil, TAG, DAG, PA, and total fatty acids, whereas overexpression decreased them. 2. PHL7 directly bound a P1BS motif in the SDP1 promoter and activated SDP1 expression. 3. PA bound PHL7 and inhibited its promoter binding, revealing lipid-mediated suppression of TAG degradation. 4. K61 was required for PA binding and also for functional complementation of the seed-oil phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: PHL7 is a promising editing target for increasing seed oil content; knockout or attenuation of its SDP1-activating function could boost oil yield, but constitutive overexpression is undesirable. Fine-tuning PA-sensitive or seed-specific alleles may enable trait improvement with fewer growth penalties.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: PHL7 overexpression caused faster early germination but smaller seedlings and shorter roots; excessive TAG degradation may impair post-germination growth
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PHL7 did not show a major seedling phosphate-starvation phenotype unlike PHR1; K61 is required for both PA interaction and DNA binding, providing a mechanistic basis for PA-mediated inhibition.
